name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022
description: ISO/IEC 27001 is an international standard for information security management systems (ISMS). It provides requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an ISMS.

categories.name: Organizational Controls
categories.description: Controls related to organizational policies, roles, responsibilities, and management of information security.

controls.name: Policies for Information Security
controls.description: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties.

requirements.description: Define and approve an information security policy at the highest level of the organization.
requirements.guidance: Ensure the policy is signed by top management and includes security objectives aligned with business strategy.

requirements.description: Communicate the policy to all relevant personnel and stakeholders.
requirements.guidance: Use multiple channels (email, intranet, training) and track acknowledgment.

controls.name: Information Security Roles and Responsibilities
controls.description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organization needs.

requirements.description: Define clear information security roles and responsibilities.
requirements.guidance: Document roles in job descriptions and RACI matrices.

requirements.description: Allocate responsibilities for information security across the organization.
requirements.guidance: Assign specific security responsibilities to appropriate personnel at all levels.

controls.name: Segregation of Duties
controls.description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.

requirements.description: Identify duties and responsibilities that need to be segregated.
requirements.guidance: Analyze processes to identify potential conflicts of interest.

requirements.description: Implement controls to enforce segregation of duties.
requirements.guidance: Use technical controls and organizational measures to prevent one person from having conflicting responsibilities.

controls.name: Management Responsibilities
controls.description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established policies and procedures.

requirements.description: Ensure management actively supports information security.
requirements.guidance: Document management commitment and include security in performance reviews.

controls.name: Threat Intelligence
controls.description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analyzed to produce threat intelligence.

requirements.description: Establish processes to collect threat intelligence from relevant sources.
requirements.guidance: Subscribe to threat feeds, participate in ISACs, and monitor security advisories.

requirements.description: Analyze threat intelligence and integrate findings into security processes.
requirements.guidance: Use threat intelligence to inform risk assessments and security controls.

controls.name: Information Security in Project Management
controls.description: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

requirements.description: Include information security requirements in project management methodologies.
requirements.guidance: Add security checkpoints and requirements to project gates.

categories.name: People Controls
categories.description: Controls related to human resource security, including hiring, during employment, and termination.

controls.name: Screening
controls.description: Background verification checks on all candidates for employment shall be carried out prior to joining the organization.

requirements.description: Conduct background checks appropriate to the role and information access.
requirements.guidance: Verify identity, qualifications, and conduct appropriate criminal and reference checks.

controls.name: Terms and Conditions of Employment
controls.description: Employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security.

requirements.description: Include information security responsibilities in employment contracts.
requirements.guidance: Reference security policies, acceptable use policies, and confidentiality requirements.

controls.name: Information Security Awareness, Education and Training
controls.description: Personnel and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training.

requirements.description: Provide security awareness training to all personnel.
requirements.guidance: Include regular training on security policies, threats, and best practices.

requirements.description: Provide role-specific security training where appropriate.
requirements.guidance: Deliver specialized training for IT staff, developers, and administrators.

controls.name: Disciplinary Process
controls.description: A disciplinary process shall be formalized and communicated to take actions against personnel who have committed an information security policy violation.

requirements.description: Define and communicate a disciplinary process for security violations.
requirements.guidance: Document the process, ensure it is fair, and align with HR policies.

controls.name: Responsibilities After Termination or Change of Employment
controls.description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated.

requirements.description: Define post-employment security obligations.
requirements.guidance: Include confidentiality obligations and intellectual property requirements in termination agreements.

categories.name: Physical Controls
categories.description: Controls related to physical security of facilities, equipment, and information.

controls.name: Physical Security Perimeters
controls.description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

requirements.description: Define physical security perimeters for sensitive areas.
requirements.guidance: Use walls, access-controlled doors, and barriers to create secure zones.

controls.name: Physical Entry
controls.description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls to ensure only authorized personnel are allowed access.

requirements.description: Implement physical access controls for secure areas.
requirements.guidance: Use badge readers, biometrics, or security personnel to control entry.

requirements.description: Maintain access logs for secure areas.
requirements.guidance: Record entry and exit times for all personnel accessing secure areas.

controls.name: Securing Offices, Rooms and Facilities
controls.description: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented.

requirements.description: Design physical security measures appropriate to the sensitivity of information.
requirements.guidance: Consider visibility, access controls, and protection against environmental threats.

controls.name: Physical Security Monitoring
controls.description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.

requirements.description: Implement monitoring systems for physical security.
requirements.guidance: Use CCTV, intrusion detection systems, and security patrols.

categories.name: Technological Controls
categories.description: Controls related to technology, including access control, cryptography, and secure development.

controls.name: User Endpoint Devices
controls.description: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

requirements.description: Implement security controls for user endpoint devices.
requirements.guidance: Deploy endpoint protection, encryption, and mobile device management.

controls.name: Privileged Access Rights
controls.description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

requirements.description: Restrict allocation of privileged access rights.
requirements.guidance: Apply least privilege principle and require approval for privileged access.

requirements.description: Monitor and review privileged access usage.
requirements.guidance: Implement privileged access management (PAM) and regular access reviews.

controls.name: Information Access Restriction
controls.description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.

requirements.description: Restrict access to information based on need-to-know.
requirements.guidance: Implement role-based access control and data classification.

controls.name: Secure Authentication
controls.description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.

requirements.description: Implement secure authentication mechanisms.
requirements.guidance: Use multi-factor authentication for sensitive systems and remote access.

requirements.description: Manage authentication credentials securely.
requirements.guidance: Enforce strong passwords, secure storage, and regular rotation.

controls.name: Management of Technical Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained, the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities evaluated and appropriate measures taken.

requirements.description: Identify technical vulnerabilities in systems.
requirements.guidance: Conduct regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing.

requirements.description: Remediate vulnerabilities based on risk.
requirements.guidance: Prioritize patching based on severity and exposure, track remediation progress.

controls.name: Configuration Management
controls.description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.

requirements.description: Establish and document secure configurations.
requirements.guidance: Define baselines for systems, applications, and network devices.

requirements.description: Monitor configurations for unauthorized changes.
requirements.guidance: Implement configuration management tools and change detection.

controls.name: Data Leakage Prevention
controls.description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.

requirements.description: Implement data loss prevention controls.
requirements.guidance: Deploy DLP tools to monitor and prevent unauthorized data transfers.

controls.name: Logging
controls.description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.

requirements.description: Implement comprehensive logging.
requirements.guidance: Log security events, access attempts, and system activities.

requirements.description: Protect log integrity and availability.
requirements.guidance: Secure logs from tampering and ensure sufficient retention.

controls.name: Monitoring Activities
controls.description: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

requirements.description: Monitor systems for anomalous behavior.
requirements.guidance: Use SIEM, IDS/IPS, and behavioral analytics to detect threats.

controls.name: Use of Cryptography
controls.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

requirements.description: Define cryptography policy and standards.
requirements.guidance: Specify approved algorithms, key lengths, and use cases.

requirements.description: Implement cryptographic key management.
requirements.guidance: Establish procedures for key generation, distribution, storage, and destruction.

controls.name: Secure Development Life Cycle
controls.description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

requirements.description: Integrate security into the development lifecycle.
requirements.guidance: Implement secure coding standards, code reviews, and security testing.

controls.name: Secure Coding
controls.description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

requirements.description: Apply secure coding practices.
requirements.guidance: Train developers on OWASP top 10, use static analysis tools.
